easyReadonly.title: easy-readonly
easyReadonly.description: Make T readonly. So, make mapped type readonly.
easyReadonly.hint: Mapped type: [K in keyof T]. Set modifiers, such as infer, before brackets.

mediumNonNullable.title: MyNonNullable
mediumNonNullable.description: Write Conditional Type that removes null and undefined from T.
mediumNonNullable.hint: Conditional Type: T extends null | undefined ? never : T

mediumReturntype.title: MyReturnType
mediumReturntype.description: Write a type that extracts the return type of function `T` (infer).
mediumReturntype.hint: infer R allows you to "capture" the type in a conditional expression. It follows the pattern (...:[]) => R

hardReadonlyPartial.title: ReadonlyPartial
hardReadonlyPartial.description: Create a type that makes all properties of object T both readonly and optional.
hardReadonlyPartial.hint: Both modifiers can be applied together: readonly [K in keyof T]?

easyRequired.title: MyRequired
easyRequired.description: Remove optionality from all properties of T. Make every field required.
easyRequired.hint: Use the -? modifier after the mapped key bracket to strip the optional flag: [K in keyof T]-?

easyExclude.title: MyExclude
easyExclude.description: Remove from union type T all members that are assignable to U.
easyExclude.hint: Conditional type: T extends U ? never : T — distribute over each union member.

mediumExtract.title: MyExtract
mediumExtract.description: Construct a type by extracting from T all union members that are assignable to U.
mediumExtract.hint: Opposite of Exclude: T extends U ? T : never

mediumPick.title: MyPick
mediumPick.description: Construct a type by picking a set of properties K from object type T.
mediumPick.hint: Constrain K with `extends keyof T`, then build a mapped type: [P in K]: T[P]

hardOmit.title: MyOmit
hardOmit.description: Construct a type by removing properties K from object type T. Combine mapped types with key remapping.
hardOmit.hint: Use `as` key remapping inside a mapped type to filter keys: [P in keyof T as P extends K ? never : P]

easyMutable.title: Mutable
easyMutable.description: Remove the readonly modifier from all properties of T. The opposite of Readonly.
easyMutable.hint: Use -readonly before the mapped key to strip the readonly modifier: -readonly [K in keyof T]

easyRecord.title: MyRecord
easyRecord.description: Create a type with keys of type K (constrained to string) and values of type V.
easyRecord.hint: Constrain K with `extends string`, then map: [P in K]: V

mediumAwaited.title: MyAwaited
mediumAwaited.description: Extract the type that a Promise resolves to. Unwrap Promise<T> into T.
mediumAwaited.hint: Use conditional type with infer: T extends Promise<infer U> ? U : T

mediumParameters.title: MyParameters
mediumParameters.description: Extract the parameter types of a function type T as a tuple.
mediumParameters.hint: Pattern: T extends (...args: infer P) => any ? P : never

aiExam.answerLabel: Write your answer to the question:

asyncSorter.failedSave: Failed to save results to DB

reverseCodeReview.selectCase: Select a case from the sidebar.

c001.title: Leaky Subscription
c001.msg0: Subscription without unsubscribe in ngOnDestroy. Use takeUntilDestroyed(), async pipe, or manual cleanup.
c001.msg1: interval() never completes. Without unsubscribe, this runs indefinitely even after component is destroyed.

c002.title: Template Traps
c002.msg0: Method call in template triggers on every change detection cycle. Use a computed property or pipe instead.
c002.msg1: formatDate() in template recalculates on every CD cycle. Use DatePipe or pre-compute the value.
c002.msg2: Method call in template runs on every change detection. Cache the result in a property or use a pipe.

c003.title: Unsafe Bindings
c003.msg0: [innerHTML] with unsanitized user content can lead to XSS attacks.Use DomSanitizer or sanitize server-side.
c003.msg1: Hardcoded API key in client-side code. Secrets are visible in browser dev tools and source bundles.

c004.title: The Subscribe Pyramid
c004.msg0: Nested subscribes form a "callback pyramid". Use switchMap/concatMap to flatten the observable chain.
c004.msg1: Nested subscriptions without cleanup. Each inner subscribe creates a leak that is never unsubscribed.

c005.title: DOM Manipulation
c005.msg0: Direct DOM access via document.getElementById bypasses Angular's abstraction. Use @ViewChild and Renderer2.
c005.msg1: Direct DOM manipulation with querySelector. Use Angular's @ViewChild, Renderer2, or host bindings instead.

c006.title: The Any Kingdom
c006.msg0: Using "any" for both parameter and return type removes all type safety. Define proper interfaces.
c006.msg1: Function signature uses "any" everywhere. Create typed interfaces for input and output.
c006.msg2: Array of "any" defeats TypeScript's purpose. Define an interface with the expected shape.

c007.title: List Rendering Issues
c007.msg0: Missing trackBy function in *ngFor. Without it, Angular re-creates all DOM elements on every change.
c007.msg1: getColor() method call in template triggers on every change detection cycle.Use ngClass + property instead.
c007.msg2: calculateTotal() runs filter on every CD cycle. Use a computed property or pipe to cache the result.

c008.title: Manual Change Detection
c008.msg0: setInterval without cleanup in ngOnDestroy. The interval keeps running after component is destroyed.
c008.msg1: Manual detectChanges() inside setInterval is an anti-pattern. Use async pipe with an Observable timer.

c009.title: Unsafe Data Handling
c009.msg0: Rendering server response as innerHTML without sanitization. Attacker could inject HTML/JS via the API.
c009.msg1: Building URL with string concatenation from user input. Use HttpParams to prevent injection.

c010.title: Shared State Mutation
c010.msg0: Direct mutation of shared state array with push(). Other won't detect the change. Use immutable updates.
c010.msg1: console.log in a getter runs on every access, lower performance. Remove debug logging from production code.
c010.msg2: Array.splice mutates the array in place. Components using OnPush won't detect this change.

c011.title: Router Event Leak
c011.msg0: Router events subscription without unsubscribe. Use takeUntilDestroyed() or manual cleanup in ngOnDestroy.
c011.msg1: Authentication token hardcoded in client-side code. Use an HTTP interceptor or environment config instead.

c012.title: Async Pipe Misuse
c012.msg0: getUsers() in template creates a new request on every change of cycle. Store the observable in a property.
c012.msg1: sec. getUsers() creates yet another subscription and request. Reuse a single observable with shareReplay.
c012.msg2: Using "any[]" loses type safety. Define a User interface with the expected properties.

c013.title: Zone.js Overload
c013.msg0: Direct DOM access with document.querySelector. Use @ViewChild to get the canvas element reference.
c013.msg1: requestAnimationFrame inside NgZone triggers change detection ~60 times/sec.Use NgZone.runOutsideAngular().

c014.title: Unsafe Form Handling
c014.msg0: [innerHTML] renders msg without sanitization. Could be exploited for XSS if the server reflects user input.
c014.msg1: Email is passed in URL query string. Credentials in URLs are logged by proxies. Use request body instead.

c015.title: Lifecycle Misuse
c015.msg0: Using "any" for component data. Define a typed interface for the user object.
c015.msg1: ngOnChanges calls loadUser() without checking which input changed. First call also duplicates ngOnInit.
c015.msg2: subscription inside a method called on every input change, prev. requests are not cancelled: use switchMap.
